issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

After trying to start the application I got the error described on: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/17248

So I executed following command on the db as suggested:
`update realm set admin_theme = 'keycloak' where admin_theme is null`

This worked and now my server starts, but when I log in on the admin section, I get an error:
2023-02-27 15:48:30,310 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-7) Uncaught server error: 
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.theme.Theme.getImportName()" because "theme" is null

	at io.quarkus.vertx.http.runtime.VertxHttpRecorder$6.handle(VertxHttpRecorder.java:408)

Can this also be corrected in v21.0.1?
 

### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

After the upgrade you should be able to log into the admin console and manage your realms, clients, identity providers...

They are visible in the dropdown to the left, but on the right the error message is shown due to the theme not being found.

![Source_code](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/68843812/221610758-a2c1dd56-7203-4497-a46c-7bbe69cb9c51.png)


### How to Reproduce?

```
    private Theme findTheme(String name, Theme.Type type) {
        for (ThemeProvider p : getProviders()) {
            if (p.hasTheme(name, type)) {
                try {
                    return p.getTheme(name, type);
                } catch (IOException e) {
                    log.errorv(e, p.getClass() + " failed to load theme, type={0}, name={1}", type, name);
                }
            }
        }
        return null;
    }
```
So maybe getProviders() is empty or p.getTheme(name, type) returns null

issue_title: Disabling features with disabled dependencies fails "Feature account2 depends on disabled feature account-api"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I'm building Keycloak as part of a docker container build and the below command fails:
bin/kc.sh build \
--http-relative-path=/auth \
--features=admin-api,admin-fine-grained-authz,admin2,js-adapter \
--features-disabled=**account-api,account2**,authorization,ciba,client-policies,client-secret-rotation,declarative-user-profile,docker,dynamic-scopes,fips,impersonation,kerberos,map-storage,openshift-integration,par,preview,recovery-codes,scripts,step-up-authentication,token-exchange,update-email,web-authn

with:
```
2023-02-28 15:27:17,657 TRACE [io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader] (main) Class quarkus.properties not found in parent first load from java.net.URLClassLoader@6aeb35e6

2023-02-28 15:27:17,657 TRACE [io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader] (main) Class quarkus.properties not found in parent first load from java.net.URLClassLoader@6aeb35e6

ERROR: Failed to run 'build' command.
ERROR: Feature account2 depends on disabled feature account-api
ERROR: Feature account2 depends on disabled feature account-api
For more details run the same command passing the '--verbose' option. Also you can use '--help' to see the details about the usage of the particular command.
```

Both features in the error messages are in the --features-disabled argument (as highlighted).

I traced the code to the keycloak-common project in the class org.keycloak.common.Profile and it seams the below check to make sure dependencies are correct is failing.
```
    private static void verifyConfig(Map<Feature, Boolean> features) {
        for (Feature f : features.keySet()) {
            if (f.getDependencies() != null) {
                for (Feature d : f.getDependencies()) {
                    if (!features.get(d)) {
                        throw new ProfileException("Feature " + f.getKey() + " depends on disabled feature " + d.getKey());
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
```
The if statement on the 3rd line should check if the feature itself is enabled before continuing with the checks.  


On a side note, the message in the error output to use --verbose should be removed.  add --verbose to the command outputs 
`Unknown option: '--verbose'`

### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

[keycloak-success-output.txt](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/10847001/keycloak-success-output.txt)


### Actual behavior

[keycloak-failed-output.txt](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/10846992/keycloak-failed-output.txt)


### How to Reproduce?

execute the following:

bin/kc.sh build \
--http-relative-path=/auth \
--features=admin-api,admin-fine-grained-authz,admin2,js-adapter \
--features-disabled=account-api,account2,authorization,ciba,client-policies,client-secret-rotation,declarative-user-profile,docker,dynamic-scopes,fips,impersonation,kerberos,map-storage,openshift-integration,par,preview,recovery-codes,scripts,step-up-authentication,token-exchange,update-email,web-authn

### Anything else?

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

Since 21.0.0 the connection string "ldaps://ldap-server-1 ldaps://ldap-server-2" is not longer supported.


### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

The connection string is still valid and will be used for failover scenarios.
When the first host is down the second one will be used

### Actual behavior

Connection string is not valid
Migration guide from 20.x does not contain a note for migrating this connection string

### How to Reproduce?

Try to use the ldap connection string "ldaps://ldap-server-1 ldaps://ldap-server-2"

### Anything else?

When the first host is down the second will be used.
If there is another way to provide this kind of "failover" feature I would use it.

Another way could be to "clone" oder "duplicate" the ldap user fedaration but there is no function for this. So all mappers must be entered twice.

Are there any plans to readd the support for failover ldap connection strings?

issue_title: User session limit make account console crash and logout the user
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

After setting the user session limits to 1 per realm and with terminate oldest session behavior, the account console crashes after a simple page refresh showing "failed to initialize keycloak" and logging out the user. In the server logs it emits the following warning message:

` type=CODE_TO_TOKEN_ERROR, realmId=myrealm, clientId=account-console, userId=null, ipAddress=172.19.0.1, error=invalid_code, grant_type=authorization_code, code_id=4103ed42-c407-476e-ae58-9860645c7d74, client_auth_method=client-secret`

If i bind again the browser flow to the built-in flow, it works.

### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

The refresh doesn't cause any logout / crash.

### Actual behavior

The user is logged out and the account console app crashes.

### How to Reproduce?

Setup the user session limits by following the [documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_user_session_limits). This means that:
- The limiter has to be configured with all the required flows
- Alternative steps/subflows must be wrapped into subflows to avoid ALTERNATIVE - REQUIRED at the same level

To check if I misconfigured / wrapped the flows, I provide the JSON of the imported realm at: [https://pastebin.com/uASeP5bz](https://pastebin.com/uASeP5bz)

### Anything else?

I had the same bug also with version 20 and 19.
commit_message:
Clarify user session limits documentation and test SSO scenario (#19372)

Closes #17374


Co-authored-by: andymunro <48995441+andymunro@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: Features guide does not work on Windows
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

The Features guide [instructs](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/7899c6c80adebebc651688c9e2b7741b18f99d82/docs/guides/src/main/server/features.adoc?plain=1#L15) the users to use `-features=<name>[,<name>]` pattern which doesn't work on Windows as `,` is taken as a [parameters delimiter](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16188#issuecomment-1372477471).

This issue is closely similar to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16188 and fix should be similar to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/16298.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Guide works on Win

### Actual behavior

Guide doesn't work on Win

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the steps on Win

### Area

### Describe the bug

I have set the Crds for a deployment, and enabled Health using the Additional Options. 

The pod starts ok, but ends up in a continuous restart loop as the readiness probe is never satisfied

issue_title: Keycloak 21.0.1 - Paging and filtering not working in "Assign roles" popup"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the new Admin UI, in "Role Mapping" tab for a user, when clicking on "Assign roles" button, the popup will display only the first page of role results. Clicking on next, will display the same first page of results, so its not possible to display subsequent pages.

When entering search term in "Search by role name" field, the search term doesn't affect the result, but the same unfiltered first page of results is displayed.

The requests sent to keycloak server: 
- /role-mappings/realm/available?first=**0**&max=11&search=ROLE_USER - result contains first 51 roles (I have 60 in the realm), but the UI displays only first 10
- /role-mappings/realm/available?first=**20**&max=11&search=ROLE_USER - result contains first 51 roles (I have 60 in the realm), but the UI displays only first 10, so the "first" and "search" parameter are not taken into account at all.

Changing page size to 100, will display all 51 roles that were loaded, but that's not complete list of roles. If there are more then 51 roles int he realm, the remaining roles above 51 are not displayed.

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

- Clicking on next should display next page instead of first page only
- Entering search term should display only matching roles

### Actual behavior

- Clicking on next shows first page only
- Entering search term has no effect on search results (non-matching roles are retrieved)

### How to Reproduce?

- Add more then 10 roles to the realm
- See that paging and filtering on "Assign roles" popup don't work

### Anything else?

This is pretty much a blocker for us for upgrading to version 21. In version 20 the issue also exists, but then at least we had an option to switch back to old admin UI. Now that the old admin UI has been removed, there is no workaround for us any more.

There is a robots.txt which prevents crawling but not indexing and it is not customizable

Access /robots.txt on the keycloak host

issue_title: Redirect loop with authentication success but access denied at default identity provider
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Currently it can happen to have infinite loop in the browser with the scenario like this:
- Keycloak is configured to use IDP `foo` . Assume it is OAuth/OIDC IDP
- Keycloak has "Identity Provider Authenticator" configured to use "Default IDP" `foo`, so it automatically redirects to this IDP when authentication is requested
- Let's assume that user wants to authenticate to Keycloak. He was already authenticated to `foo` provider in his browser
- Keycloak redirects to IDP automatically (due "Identity Provider Authenticator"), but user doesn't have some needed permission in the `foo` provider. So `foo` IDP will return `error=access_denied` .
- Keycloak is currently configured that when it receives `error=access_denied` from 3rd-party IDP, it starts the authentication again. But in this case, this will cause another redirection to `foo` (due the "Identity Provider Authenticator").

So we have infinite loop here

## Note

There was similar issue already fixed some time ago https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-17368, but that didn't handled the `access_denied` error. This error is currently treated in different way than most other errors in Keycloak - see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/oidc/AbstractOAuth2IdentityProvider.java#L493-L494 .

Why is `error=access_denied` treated this way? The OAuth2 specification mentions that this error means `The resource owner or authorization server denied the request.` . However Keycloak currently always assumes that `error=access_denied` means that user (resource owner) himself rejected consent screen on the IDP side. For the case when IDP itself (authorization server) rejected authentication (for example due some missing permission), the behaviour is not very nice as user will see the Keycloak username/password screen without any message shown to him that he should authenticate the other way due access-denied from server. Also Keycloak doesn't handle the case with default-idp set, which can mean this infinite loop

## Fix proposal

To fix the infinite loop, we can display some sensible message like `Access denied when authenticating to My-IDP` and let the user authenticate some other way (like username/password screen). This is what we're currently doing for handling other errors from IDP. This means that "Identity Provider Authenticator" won't try to redirect to "default IDP" for the 2nd time when it sees that previous authentication to IDP failed.

In case that administrator requires that authentication is handled by this IDP, he can switch `Forms` authenticator as `DISABLED` in the default browser flow. In this case, the error message would be displayed and user can go back to the client application (he cannot fallback to username/password screen).

## Future thoughts

This fix should be sufficient for most of the cases as people usually have issues exactly with this `error=access_denied`. But we can eventually have more flexible error handling for Identity brokering.

For example we can have some SPI for how to handle various error messages sent by OIDC/SAML IDPs. We can do various actions like:
- Restart authentication (pretty much what we currently do now)
- Display error directly
- Redirect error to the client (will need to handle cases when IDP and client are different protocols - like IDP is OIDC, but client is SAML protocol and/or vice-versa)

### Version

21.0.1
commit_message:
Redirect loop with authentication success but access denied at default identity provider
closes #17441

issue_title: Bug in SAML Redirect Binding with 2 validating certificates
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

If I configure a SAML-IdentityProvider under Menu -> IdentityProvider and use 2 "Validating X509 Certificates" separated with comma (",") and use Redirect-Binding for IDP Responses I get an error:
Caused by: org.keycloak.common.VerificationException: Invalid key locator for signature verification
        at org.keycloak.protocol.saml.SamlProtocolUtils.verifyRedirectSignature(SamlProtocolUtils.java:183)


So we have two certificates with MIIH1DCCBbygAwIBAgITbQASIUmhHRspDof1LAACABI...,MIIHxjCCBa6gAwIBAgITbQAU15owiFXb0lHXUgACABTXmj... 
Note the comma in the middle. This is possible to support signing certificate change of the IDP.
So the assumption is that both certificates are checked for signature validation. This works in the case of POST-Binding, but not in the case of REDIRECT-Binding.
I inspected the code in this area and I found the bug in this case. The verify signature method is a different implementation in case of POST-Binding and Redirect-Binding.
Redirect-Binding uses this Method:
org/keycloak/protocol/saml/SamlProtocolUtils.java:178
There is org.keycloak.rotation.HardcodedKeyLocator#getKey Method used, which implements this method:

public Key getKey(String kid) {
    if (this.keys.size() == 1) {
        return this.keys.iterator().next();
    } else {
        return null;
    }
}

So only in the case we have only one key the implementation works. In the case we have two separated keys it won't work.
The correct implementation is to iterate over the HardcodedKeyLocator like it does the POST-Binding Implementation in this area:
org/keycloak/saml/processing/core/util/XMLSignatureUtil.java:498

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

No Exception when using 2 certifiactes

### Actual behavior

If I configure a SAML-IdentityProvider under Menu -> IdentityProvider and use 2 "Validating X509 Certificates" separated with comma (",") and use Redirect-Binding for IDP Responses I get an error:
Caused by: org.keycloak.common.VerificationException: Invalid key locator for signature verification
        at org.keycloak.protocol.saml.SamlProtocolUtils.verifyRedirectSignature(SamlProtocolUtils.java:183)

### How to Reproduce?

If I configure a SAML-IdentityProvider under Menu -> IdentityProvider and use 2 "Validating X509 Certificates" separated with comma (",") and use Redirect-Binding for IDP Responses I get an error:
Caused by: org.keycloak.common.VerificationException: Invalid key locator for signature verification
        at org.keycloak.protocol.saml.SamlProtocolUtils.verifyRedirectSignature(SamlProtocolUtils.java:183)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: CVE-2023-24815 - Directory Traversal vulnerability in io.vertx:vertx-web
issue_body:
### Description

**Note**: The issue affects the Keycloak Operator and the Quarkus distribution

-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: io.vertx:vertx-web
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:[keycloak-operator@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-operator@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT), io.quarkiverse.operatorsdk:quarkus-operator-sdk@4.0.7 and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:[keycloak-operator@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-operator@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) › io.quarkiverse.operatorsdk:quarkus-operator-sdk@4.0.7 › io.quarkus:[quarkus-smallrye-health@2.13.7.Final](mailto:quarkus-smallrye-health@2.13.7.Final) › io.quarkus:[quarkus-vertx-http@2.13.7.Final](mailto:quarkus-vertx-http@2.13.7.Final) › io.vertx:vertx-web@4.3.4

# Overview

[io.vertx:vertx-web](https://github.com/vert-x3/vertx-web) is a HTTP web applications for Vert.x.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal when running Vertx web applications that serve files using `StaticHandle`. If the mount point is a wildcard (`*`) then an attacker can exfiltrate any class path resource.

When computing the relative path to locate the resource, in case of wildcards, [the code](https://github.com/vert-x3/vertx-web/blob/62c0d66fa1c179ae6a4d57344631679a2b97e60f/vertx-web/src/main/java/io/vertx/ext/web/impl/Utils.java#L83) returns the user input (without validation) as the segment to lookup. Even though checks are performed to avoid escaping the sandbox, given that the input was not sanitized, `\` are not properly handled and an attacker can build a path that is valid within the classpath.

**Note:** This vulnerability can only be exploited on Windows Operating Systems and Windows File Systems

# Details

A Directory Traversal attack (also known as path traversal) aims to access files and directories that are stored outside the intended folder. By manipulating files with "dot-dot-slash (../)" sequences and its variations, or by using absolute file paths, it may be possible to access arbitrary files and directories stored on file system, including application source code, configuration, and other critical system files.

Directory Traversal vulnerabilities can be generally divided into two types:

-   **Information Disclosure**: Allows the attacker to gain information about the folder structure or read the contents of sensitive files on the system.

`st` is a module for serving static files on web pages, and contains a [vulnerability of this type](https://snyk.io/vuln/npm:st:20140206). In our example, we will serve files from the `public` route.

If an attacker requests the following URL from our server, it will in turn leak the sensitive private key of the root user.

```
curl http://localhost:8080/public/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/root/.ssh/id_rsa
```

**Note** `%2e` is the URL encoded version of `.` (dot).

-   **Writing arbitrary files**: Allows the attacker to create or replace existing files. This type of vulnerability is also known as `Zip-Slip`.

One way to achieve this is by using a malicious `zip` archive that holds path traversal filenames. When each filename in the zip archive gets concatenated to the target extraction folder, without validation, the final path ends up outside of the target folder. If an executable or a configuration file is overwritten with a file containing malicious code, the problem can turn into an arbitrary code execution issue quite easily.

The following is an example of a `zip` archive with one benign file and one malicious file. Extracting the malicious file will result in traversing out of the target folder, ending up in `/root/.ssh/` overwriting the `authorized_keys` file:

```
2018-04-15 22:04:29 .....           19           19  good.txt
2018-04-15 22:04:42 .....           20           20  ../../../../../../root/.ssh/authorized_keys
```

# Remediation

Upgrade `io.vertx:vertx-web` to version 4.3.8 or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/vert-x3/vertx-web/commit/8a6b4ef8096a0b8714c1f10ec0133f4b42e48428)
-   [PoC](https://github.com/adrien-aubert-drovio/vertx-statichandler-windows-traversal-path-vulnerability)
-   [Vulnerable Code](https://github.com/vert-x3/vertx-web/blob/62c0d66fa1c179ae6a4d57344631679a2b97e60f/vertx-web/src/main/java/io/vertx/ext/web/impl/Utils.java#L83)

issue_title: SAML2 Client Signing Keys Config does not accept PEM import
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When I want to create a new SAML2 client in Keycloak and I want to require client signatures, I can click `Regenerate` and receive a private key. The conceptual problem is that
a) keycloak should never know the private key
b) I don't want to transfer the private key to my client. 
Private keys are private. They should be generated on the client and remain on the client. What I actually want to do is to import the client's public certificate as trusted.
This behaviour is broken since the Admin-UI redesign.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5484682/223795230-3951c066-dbfb-40a1-85f8-d33fced14aa3.png)

When I click on `Import Key` the dropdown seems to be broken:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5484682/223795473-7ea9c6b5-4775-4e8c-af1f-4f3b05565cbe.png)
I want either .crt or .pem import, but the only options available are JKS and PKCS12.

I can create a PKCS12 file on my client without a private key:
````
└─$ openssl pkcs12 -export -nokeys -in public.crt -name foo -out foo.p12                                         1 ⨯
Enter Export Password:
Verifying - Enter Export Password:
````
But if I import this in keycloak I get the following error:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5484682/223802167-66323e79-058a-434c-b599-612ccba47011.png)

What am I missing here? Keycloak does not need the client's private key, so why is this new dialog forcing me to give it one?


### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

I can import a trusted cert from the client

### Actual behavior

I have to provide a private key or use the private key which keycloak generates, which does not make sense from a security perspective

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start fresh keycloak (e.g. use the getting-started-docker guide)
2. Create new Realm
3. Create new SAML2 client
4. Go to `Keys` Tab of the client configuration
5. Try to import a .pem or .crt public certificate from the client

### Anything else?

issue_title: Stepup issue on "remember_me" authentication : alreadyLoggedIn
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

We made a jump in version from KC 17 to KC 20.
Since then we noticed an increasing number of users complaining the apparition of the alreadyLoggedIn message.

The bug appears with a "remember me" session, and when after the user restarts its browser (deleting session cookie as AUTH_SESSION_ID but keeping the KEYCLOAK_IDENTITY one since it's a persistent cookie due to "remember_me").
If the authentication flow contains an authenticator that trigger a form, then the submit of this form results in the display of on a "alreadylogin" message.

This can be shown on a with standard stepup flow at the OTP step (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_step-up-flow)


### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

On a "remember me" session,
When the browser is closed and reopened (cleaning session cookies but keeping persistent cookies) 
And the user triggers an authentication flow needing a user interaction (like in stepup flow)

Then the authentication flows is not blocked by an "alreadyLoggedIn" error message



### Actual behavior

On a "remember me" session,
When the browser is closed and reopened (cleaning session cookies but keeping persistent cookies) 
And the user triggers an authentication flow needing a user interaction (like in stepup flow)

Then an alreadyLoggedIn error message is displayed

### How to Reproduce?

The bug appears when the authentication flow contains an authenticator that needs a user interaction (a form  to be submited)

Prerequisite : 
the STEPUP flow is enabled ([see ](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_step-up-flow))


1 - the user authenticates itself with the "remember me" feature
2 - the user close all instances of his browser 
3 - the user opens his browser  
4 - the user triggers a stepup auth (via the request parameter acr_values=2 in the oidc authentication request for instance) 
5 - The OTP is then required 
6 - When the user submit the form the OTP the error message is displayed

### Anything else?

issue_title: SAML Signature metadata loses certificate info
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

When option `signSpMetadata` is enabled for a SAML IdP, the SAML SP metadata endpoint always exposes the public key as a `RSAKeyValue` element, even if key material was passed as an X.509 certificate. This means that crucial information like the issuer is lost, and hampers the ability to establish a chain of trust when validating the metadata signature.

Notably, this behavior is also inconsistent: With `wantAssertionsEncrypted` enabled, the same key material is presented as `X509Data`, ie. retaining the certificate, within the `SPSSODescriptor` element.

This issue was seen on Keycloak 18 and reproduced with current Keycloak 21, but seems to be present since the addition of the metadata signing feature in Keycloak 12 (commit 10077b1efe1490bc91db2816be304b0d53f09301)

### Version

21

### Expected behavior

Metadata info as presented from `broker/saml/endpoint/descriptor` endpoint contains a KeyInfo node like

```xml
<dsig:KeyInfo>
  <ds:X509Data>
    <ds:X509Certificate>MIIC(...)</ds:X509Certificate>
  </ds:X509Data>
</dsig:KeyInfo>
```

in its `Signature` node if metadata signing is enabled.

### Actual behavior

Metadata info as presented from `broker/saml/endpoint/descriptor` endpoint contains a KeyInfo node like

```xml
<dsig:KeyInfo>
  <dsig:KeyValue>
    <dsig:RSAKeyValue>
      <dsig:Modulus>(...)</dsig:Modulus>
      <dsig:Exponent>ABCD</dsig:Exponent>
    </dsig:RSAKeyValue>
  </dsig:KeyValue>
</dsig:KeyInfo>
```

in its `Signature` node if metadata signing is enabled.

### How to Reproduce?

* Add SAML IdP
* Enable option `Sign Service Provider Metadata`
* Check `Signature` node in SAML 2.0 Service Provider Metadata endpoint

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Prefer cert over pubkey in SAML metadata

If SAML key material was given as a certificate, consistently
expose the certificate rather than just the public key when
presenting SAML metadata info. This change ensures that the
client obtains sufficient information (eg. issuer) to close
the trust chain.

Closes: #17549

Signed-off-by: Daniel Kobras <kobras@puzzle-itc.de>
